title: Critical Angle and Total Internal Reflection

concepts.title: Critical Angle
concepts.conceptBody:
Critical Angle

When a ray of light goes from denser medium to a racer medium, the angle of refraction is greater than the angle of incidence.

From this statement, we can also observe that, in some cases the angle of refraction can increase to become 90 or more than 90 .

The angle of incidence for which the angle refraction becomes 90 is called the article angle for that interface.

It is generally denoted by c.

Let ${{\mu }_{R}}$ be the refractive index of the rarer medium and ${{\mu }_{D}}$ be the refractive index of the denser medium. Obviously, ${{\mu }_{\text{r}}}<{{\mu }_{\text{D}}}$.

Hence, from Snell's law $\frac{\text{sin}c}{\text{sin}{{90}^{\circ }}}=\frac{{{\mu }_{r}}}{{{\mu }_{D}}}$

or $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sinc}=\frac{1}{\mu }$

where $\mu =\frac{{{\mu }_{D}}}{{{\mu }_{R}}}$ is the refractive index of the denser medium with respect to the rarer medium.

concepts.title: Total Internal Reflection
concepts.conceptBody:
Total Internal Reflection 

While a light ray goes from a denser medium to a rarer medium, the angle of refraction is greater than the angle of incidence. If the angle of incidence is increased, the angle of refraction may eventually become 90 .

If a ray of light travelling in a denser medium strikes a rarer medium at an angle of incidence $\text{i}$ which is greater than the critical angle $\text{C}$, it gets totally reflected back into the same medium. This phenomenon is called as total internal reflection.

concepts.title: Application of Total Internal Reflection
concepts.conceptBody:
Application of Total Internal Reflection

1.Shining of air bubble 

An air bubble in water shines due to total internal reflection at its outer surface. Here light is propagating from denser medium (water) to rarer medium (air) and if i $>{{\theta }_{\text{C}}}\left( 49 \right)$ ) TIR will take place at the surface of the bubble. This is shown in figure (A)

2.Sparkling of diamond 

The sparkling of diamond is due to total internal reflection inside it. As refractive index for diamond is 2.5 so ${{\theta }_{C}}=24$. Now the cuttings of diamond are such that $\text{i}>{{\text{i}}_{\text{C}}}$, so TIR will take place again and again inside it. The light which beams out from a few places in some specific directions makes it sparkle. Recently developed synthetic rutile (titanium dioxide) displays even greater brilliance as for it $\mu \simeq 2.9$ and so ${{\theta }_{\text{C}}}\simeq 20$

 3.Optical fiber 

Now a days total internal reflection is used in fiber optics. In it light through multiple total internal reflections is propagated along the axis of a glass fiber of radius of few microns in which index of refraction of core ${{\mu }_{1}}\left( =1.7 \right)$ is greater than that of surroundings (called cladding) ${{\mu }_{2}}$ (= 1.5) as shown in Fig. (B). At present fiber optics is used in telecommunications for propagating signals from one place to the other as through a single fiber about 300 signals can be transmitted simultaneously without any interference whereas an electric wire can transmit only one. This is also used by doctors to visualize internal sites of the body.
